name: Marie-Blanque Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 6 : Le col de Marie-Blanque — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
Marie-Blanque is a mythical pass in the Pyrenees and a historic part of the Tour de France. Since 1978, it has been crossed fifteen times by the big loop. Uniting the beautiful valleys of Ossau and Aspe, it culminates at 1,035 meters above sea level. This rural pass owes its name to the Egyptian vulture that inhabits the region.

The start is in the town center of Louvie-Juzon, a charming village at the gates of the Ossau valley. The route plunges into the valley for four kilometers before embarking on the ascent of the pass. The first laces lead to the Bénou plateau which allows you to catch your breath before tackling the final portion of the climb.

With the exception of a first hairpin, the descent slides almost straight into the bottom of a valley which leads to the village of Escot. You will find a beautiful balcony road that winds up to Lurbe-Saint-Christau. From there, you take an uncrowded and hilly road which takes you to the gates of Louvie-Juzon. Along the way, a slight climb awaits you up to the Maquis du Bager stele.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Louvie-Juzon are situated in the Pyrenean foothills, characterized by a green, pastoral environment and views of mountain ranges like the Pic du Midi d'Ossau. The terrain offers challenging ascents, including renowned cols, alongside quiet roads winding through charming villages. This region provides diverse cycling experiences with significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Louvie-Juzon The most popular road cycling route is Aubisque-Soulor – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees, a 51.2 miles (82.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Pyrenean foothills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Marie-Blanque Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees, a difficult 31.2 miles (50.2 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and scenic views of the surrounding natural features. Local road cyclists also love the Plateau du Bénou – Marie-Blanque Pass loop from Arudy, a 24.5 miles (39.4 km) trail leading through the Plateau du Bénou, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Louvie-Juzon is defined by Pyrenean foothills, challenging mountain passes, and quiet valley roads. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Louvie-Juzon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 14,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Louvie-Juzon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Louvie-Juzon?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes around Louvie-Juzon, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find routes varying from easy to difficult, with 14 easy, 46 moderate, and 27 difficult trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Louvie-Juzon?
A: The Pyrenean foothills around Louvie-Juzon are best enjoyed for road cycling during the warmer months, typically from late spring to early autumn. This period offers more stable weather conditions and clear views of the mountain ranges, including the Pic du Midi d'Ossau.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Louvie-Juzon?
A: The terrain in Louvie-Juzon is diverse, ranging from challenging ascents through the Pyrenean foothills to quiet, small roads winding through charming villages. You can expect significant elevation gains on routes that tackle renowned cols, alongside more pastoral and green valley environments.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Louvie-Juzon?
A: Yes, Louvie-Juzon is known for its challenging road cycling routes, including significant ascents that are part of major cycling events. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064636832/aubisque-soulor-emblematic-passes-of-the-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aubisque-Soulor – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068301174/marie-blanque-pass-emblematic-passes-of-the-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees&lt;/a&gt; offer demanding climbs and substantial elevation gains.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Louvie-Juzon?
A: While the region is known for its challenging climbs, there are 14 easy road cycling routes available around Louvie-Juzon. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and provide a gentler introduction to cycling in the Pyrenean foothills, often winding through valleys and villages.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Louvie-Juzon offer breathtaking views of the Pyrenean foothills and mountain ranges, including the prominent Pic du Midi d'Ossau. You can also encounter famous mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col du Soulor&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aubisque&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features quiet roads through charming villages and pastoral scenery.

Q: Are there specific viewpoints offering panoramic views on the routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by stunning viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4635557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soulor Panorama&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive vistas, and you can find a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4199861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Petit and Grand Gabizos&lt;/a&gt;. These spots provide excellent opportunities to appreciate the dramatic Pyrenean landscape.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Louvie-Juzon?
A: Yes, there are circular road cycling routes in the area. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36613739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laruns – Col d'Aubisque loop from Louvie-Juzon&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Pyrenean foothills and back to your starting point, offering a comprehensive tour of the local scenery.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay along the cycling routes in Louvie-Juzon?
A: The quiet roads often wind through charming villages where you can find local establishments. The Ossau Valley, which many routes traverse, is known for its local farms where you can taste the famous Ossau-Iraty sheep's cheese, adding a cultural and culinary dimension to your ride. Accommodation options are available in and around Louvie-Juzon and the surrounding villages.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Louvie-Juzon?
A: The road cycling routes around Louvie-Juzon are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 270 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the breathtaking landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the tranquility of the quiet roads that allow for an immersive experience in the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Louvie-Juzon?
A: While many routes are challenging, the presence of 14 easy routes suggests options suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. These routes typically avoid the steepest climbs and focus on the quieter valley roads, offering a pleasant experience for all skill levels.

Q: Are road cycling routes in Louvie-Juzon suitable for long-distance rides?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the region are designed for longer distances and significant elevation, making them ideal for long-distance road cycling. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064636832/aubisque-soulor-emblematic-passes-of-the-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aubisque-Soulor – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees&lt;/a&gt; route covers over 82 kilometers, providing a substantial challenge for experienced riders.
tips: Louvie-Juzon is located at the gates of the magnificent Ossau valley. This charming little village is the ideal starting point for many mountain passes. From here the Marie-Blanque pass is very close, while you will have to pedal a little more to find the mythical Aubisque and Soulor passes.
Provides welcome respite between two steep sections up to Marie Blanque. If descending, enjoy the magnificent views but be alert to wandering livestock and people.

The Col de Marie-Blanque is an emblematic passage of the Tour de France which peaks at 1,035 meters above sea level. Its ascent from Louvie-Juzon is 15 kilometers long for an average of 4.1% with passages at 8.5%. Marie-Blanque marks the border between the beautiful valleys of Ossau and Aspe. In the middle of the climb, the pretty Bénou plateau offers a portion of respite in a remarkable setting.
Nice road without too much winding traffic! The coating is correct and it is partly shaded
